Package dev.jeka.core.api.utils

Class JkUtilsIterable

java.lang.Object
dev.jeka.core.api.utils.JkUtilsIterable
public final class JkUtilsIterable extends Object
Utility class for dealing with Iterable.

    Method
    Description
    static <T> void
    addAllWithoutDuplicate(Collection<T> recipientList, Iterable<? extends T> items)
    Adds items to the specified recipient list.

    Creates an array of the specified class and populate it with items of the specified Iterable.

  • Constructor Details

    • JkUtilsIterable

      public JkUtilsIterable()

  • Method Details

    • listOf

      public static <T> List<T> listOf(Iterable<T> it)
      Returns a list form the specified Iterable. If the specified Iterable is yet a List than it is returned as is, otherwise a brand new List is created.

    • listOf

      @SafeVarargs @Deprecated public static <T> List<T> listOf(T... items)
      Deprecated.
      See Also:
      • listOf(Iterable)

    • listOf2orMore

      @SafeVarargs public static <T> List<T> listOf2orMore(T item1, T item2, T... others)
      See Also:
      • listOf(Iterable)

    • listOf1orMore

      @SafeVarargs public static <T> List<T> listOf1orMore(T item1, T... others)
      See Also:
      • listOf(Iterable)

    • listWithoutDuplicateOf

      public static <T> List<T> listWithoutDuplicateOf(Iterable<T> items)
      Returns a duplicate free list of the given items

    • addAllWithoutDuplicate

      public static <T> void addAllWithoutDuplicate(Collection<T> recipientList, Iterable<? extends T> items)
      Adds items to the specified recipient list. Only item not already present in the recipient list are added.

    • arrayOf

      public static <T> T[] arrayOf(Iterable<T> it, Class<T> clazz)
      Creates an array of the specified class and populate it with items of the specified Iterable.

    • setOf

      @SafeVarargs public static <T> Set<T> setOf(T... items)
      Creates a set of specified items.

    • setOf

      public static <T> Set<T> setOf(Iterable<T> items)
      Creates a set of specified items.

    • mapOf

      public static <T, U> Map<T,U> mapOf(T key, U value, Object... others)
      Creates a map of specified key/value. Key of should be declared in sequence as mapOf(key1, value1, key2, value2,...)

    • mapOfAny

      public static <T, U> Map<T,U> mapOfAny(Object... others)
      Creates a map of specified key/value. Key of should be declared in sequence as mapOf(key1, value1, key2, value2,...)

    • putMultiEntry

      public static <K, V> void putMultiEntry(Map<K,V> map, Iterable<K> keys, V value)
      Convenient method to put several entry in a map having the same value at once.

    • concatLists

      @SafeVarargs public static <T> List<T> concatLists(Iterable<? extends T>... lists)
      Returns a list that is a concatenation of the specified lists. The result is not backed by specified Iterable.

    • propertiesToMap

      public static Map<String,String> propertiesToMap(Properties properties)
      Creates a Map and populates it with specified properties.

    • toString

      public static String toString(Map<?,?> map)
      Returns a string representation of the specified map constructed as [key0]=[value0], [key1]=[value1], ...

    • toStrings

      public static List<String> toStrings(Map<?,?> map)
      Returns a multi-line string representation of the specified map constructed as [key0]=[value0], [key1]=[value1], ...

    • concat

      public static <T> T[] concat(T[] a, T[] b)
      Concatenates two arrays

    • toMultiLineString

      public static String toMultiLineString(Iterable<?> items, String margin)

    • writeStringsTo

      public static void writeStringsTo(Path path, String delimiter, List<String> strings)

    • readStringsFrom

      public static List<String> readStringsFrom(Path path, String delimiter)

    • readStringSafelyFrom

      public static List<String> readStringSafelyFrom(Path path, String delimiter)
